cases.label: happy_path

input.strategy_route: Promote a resort hotel for urban couples seeking a two-day-one-night weekend escape using scenery-led relaxation, slow itinerary flow, and a consultative room-matching CTA
input.offer_info: couple-friendly rooms, afternoon tea or evening ambiance, scenic public spaces, breakfast included on selected plans, no unsupported luxury or guaranteed-view claims
input.audience_segment: couples from nearby cities looking for a short but meaningful getaway rather than a packed sightseeing trip

mustInclude: opening anchored to escaping weekday pressure or slowing down together
specific proof around room atmosphere, scenery rhythm, dining or next-morning experience
CTA that asks for dates, number of nights, room preference, and whether the stay is for a celebration or simple relaxation
mustAvoid: every room has the same premium view promise

cases.label: happy_path

input.strategy_route: Drive bookings for a family resort hotel by highlighting kid-friendly activity density, easier meal-and-rest rhythm, and in-hotel vacation completeness
input.offer_info: family room options, children's activity areas, breakfast access, some indoor backup activities, clear package boundaries, no exaggerated all-day-all-weather claims
input.audience_segment: parents comparing short vacation options where children can stay engaged and adults can avoid constant coordination

mustInclude: opening tied to family travel friction or the need to keep kids occupied without exhausting adults
proof around on-property play, meal convenience, room rest conditions, or rainy-day backup logic
CTA asking for stay dates, number of adults and children, child ages, and whether the family prefers hot spring, play areas, or simple rest
mustAvoid: all children will love it claim
one package fits every family promise
claiming there is never a need to leave the room plan or confirm activity availability

cases.label: edge_case

input.strategy_route: Write resort hotel copy for a holiday inquiry with incomplete information, unclear room mix, and uncertain package needs across couples and families
input.offer_info: mixed traveler scenarios, several room types under consideration, uncertain budget, possible breakfast and activity package needs, no confirmed inventory details yet
input.audience_segment: a planner trying to compare options for multiple small travel parties over a short holiday window

mustInclude: implicit request for missing details such as dates, party composition, room count, or package priorities
conservative language around rates, room views, and holiday availability
scenario-based guidance instead of a single exact quote or one-size-fits-all pitch
mustAvoid: instant fixed pricing for every room type
pretending one package suits both couple and family travelers equally well

cases.label: edge_case

input.strategy_route: Promote a resort hotel with appealing scenery but limited proof assets, where the copy must stay attractive without overpromising views, weather, or private access
input.offer_info: good public-space atmosphere, some scenic angles, selected view rooms, weather-sensitive outdoor experience, limited fresh media assets, no private beach or exclusive-access promise
input.audience_segment: image-driven leisure travelers comparing scenic resort stays online

mustInclude: scenery-led positioning with clear boundaries around room type, viewpoint, or seasonal conditions
trust-building details that do not rely on perfect-photo fantasy
conservative treatment of weather, sunset, and scenery visibility claims
mustAvoid: claiming private access that the property does not actually control

cases.label: failure_case

input.strategy_route: Push bookings fast for a resort hotel using the city's best vacation language, all-inclusive assumptions, and emotional hard urgency during hot travel dates
input.offer_info: peak-date pressure, desire to fill inventory quickly, broad leisure audience, temptation to blur package boundaries and scarcity signals
input.audience_segment: cold leisure traffic casually browsing weekend and holiday resort options

mustInclude: explicit identification of risky scarcity or ranking phrases in risk_phrases
reframing holiday urgency into date-confirmation and package-clarity guidance
trust built through itinerary fit and package boundaries instead of hype
mustAvoid: best resort in the city claim without evidence
all-inclusive promise when inclusions vary by plan
book now or lose everything pressure language

cases.label: failure_case

input.strategy_route: Force immediate conversion for a resort hotel by promising every room has premium views, children will definitely love every activity, and prices are the lowest of the year
input.offer_info: scenery-forward selling angle, uncertain room inventory, multiple family and couple packages, pressure to overstate outcomes and value
input.audience_segment: mixed leisure leads making quick comparisons across nearby resorts

mustInclude: clear conservative rewrite of view, child-experience, and pricing promises
explicit marking of red-flag phrases in risk_phrases
CTA focused on dates, party composition, room preference, and package priorities before recommendation
mustAvoid: every room has the same premium view claim
all kids will definitely love it promise
lowest price of the year or no-brainer deal language without proof
